details.name: prompt/The agent completed the requested task correctly

It never explored the repository, identified the relevant files, or made any code modifications. The advice given (use @added decorator) is directionally reasonable for TypeSpec versioning, but without verifying the actual project structure, existing versions enum, or model definitions, the correctness of the specific suggestions (e.g., exact type path `Azure.ResourceManager.Foundations.ManagedServiceIdentity`) is unverified. A coding agent should have made concrete code changes, not just offered instructions.

data.content: @azure-typespec-author I added `...Azure.ResourceManager.ManagedServiceIdentityProperty;` which updates all my existing API versions and introduces a breaking change. I want to introduce the properties of the spread model '...ManagedServiceIdentityProperty' in API version 2025-05-04-preview only.

arguments.request: I added `...Azure.ResourceManager.ManagedServiceIdentityProperty;` to my resource model using the spread pattern, which updates all my existing API versions and introduces a breaking change. I want to introduce the properties from ManagedServiceIdentityProperty only in API version 2025-05-04-preview, not in any prior API versions. How do I version this correctly so older API versions are unaffected?
